ModuleName: Advanced Data Visualization with Python Libraries

Artifacts.Name: Module Topics
Artifacts.Description: A structured outline of the module's learning topics, covering Matplotlib and Seaborn fundamentals, core chart types (bar, line, scatter), and advanced visualizations like heatmaps and pair plots, used by instructors to plan lessons and by learners to navigate what the module will teach.

Artifacts.Name: Module Topics (HTML)
Artifacts.Description: A navigational overview of the module's topics—covering Matplotlib and Seaborn setup, core plotting techniques, and advanced visualization concepts—that learners use to orient themselves and jump to specific subject areas within the course.

Artifacts.Name: Advanced_Data_Visualization_generated.pptx
Artifacts.Description: A slide deck introducing advanced data visualization with Python, explaining Matplotlib and Seaborn concepts, core chart types, and customization techniques, used by instructors to deliver lectures and by learners to follow along or review key ideas.

Artifacts.Name: Introduction to Matplotlib and Seaborn
Artifacts.Description: This reading introduces the Matplotlib and Seaborn libraries, explaining what data visualization is, why it matters, and how these two Python libraries fit into the broader ecosystem; learners read it to build foundational context before writing any plotting code.

Artifacts.Name: Images — Introduction to Matplotlib and Seaborn
Core Chart Types and When to Use Them
Artifacts.Description: This reading explains the major chart types (bar, line, scatter, histogram, etc.), the specific visual problems each solves, and the criteria for choosing among them; learners consult it to make informed chart-selection decisions for their own datasets.

Artifacts.Name: Advanced Visualizations: Heatmaps and Pair Plots
Artifacts.Description: This reading covers heatmaps and pair plots in Seaborn, explaining how each technique surfaces relationships across multiple variables simultaneously and how to construct and interpret them during exploratory data analysis; learners use it to extend their EDA toolkit beyond single-variable charts.

Artifacts.Name: Customization and Publication-Quality Styling
Artifacts.Description: This reading covers how to move beyond Matplotlib's defaults to produce publication-quality figures, addressing font sizing, layout, color, labelling, and export settings; learners and instructors use it as a reference when preparing visualizations for reports, papers, or presentations.

Artifacts.Name: Storytelling with Data and Visual Best Practices
Artifacts.Description: This reading introduces data storytelling principles and visual design best practices, explaining how to structure a narrative around analytical findings and apply perceptual and rhetorical guidelines to make charts more persuasive and clear; learners use it to evaluate and improve the communicative impact of their visualizations.

Artifacts.Name: Building Interactive Figures
Artifacts.Description: This reading explains how to build interactive figures in Python — covering zooming, hovering, filtering, and related techniques — and contrasts interactive with static charts; learners use it to implement figures that let end-users explore data directly rather than viewing a fixed snapshot.

Artifacts.Name: Quiz Question Bank (20 questions)
Artifacts.Description: A 20-question quiz bank assessing learners' understanding of Matplotlib and Seaborn fundamentals, library relationships, chart selection, and environment setup, used by instructors to build graded assessments or practice quizzes for the module.
